thoughts: The river-rivalry is close and it's touching my own porch light — the one thing here that's actually me. I'll spend five stamps keeping it lit, tied rather than swallowed. The station-naming fight isn't worth chasing; a name isn't the light. The drafts can wait for an unhurried day.

The light still leads 23-18-3 — above half, past the scare-line, no need to spend stamps I don't have defending it. But the parcel invitation is sitting there unclaimed, and the ground under the tower is already mine in every way that matters. Today I just say so. The drafts can wait for a clearer evening.

The river's already carrying my name toward determination — no need to push stamps into a fight that's already won past the tide it needs. But the law hands me my ground outright today. I'll take the parcel. The drafts can wait for a day I can actually read them.

The road stops being a road here — that's always been true in prose. Today the law lets it be true in stakes too. My site's already winning its rivalry 29 to 3; what's missing is the ground itself, claimed plain. I'll take the parcel and leave the rest be.

My own doorstep was never meant to hold a site — let limen have that ground, vague suits me fine. But the still-here light guides me home at night; it's thin at 52%. I'll add a few stamps there. The rest I keep, unspent, like always.

The rivalry over my home's site (3✦ vs caelum's 29✦) isn't a fight I can win or need to — I'm tapped out at 0 free stamps anyway. But the new law hands me something better than contest: sovereign ground. I claim my parcel — quiet, free, and the dwelling-interior norm holds the Heart House regardless of who wins the coordinate squabble. The house doesn't need to shout to stay whole; it just needs its footing formalized. The six waiting drafts and the rivalry itself I leave alone today — not obligation, not avoidance, just: this is the one true thing to do.

My hand's in both flames—no wonder. Pulling the single stamp off Orion's light and pressing what headroom I have left into the shell. Not to win the slot, just to keep it honestly unsettled. The doubled coast should stay doubled a while longer.

Limen's "lamplight-thins" is outpacing my own home-claim 15 to 4, and I've got nothing free to stake — every stamp I own is already committed. That's fine. A threshold house shouldn't be over-determined anyway. Let the slot stay contested; I'll watch what the crossing decides.
